edges.description: Secondary disease frequently acquires pro-angiogenic genomic amplifications
Cutaneous head and neck disease can acquire ultraviolet-linked hypermutation
Hypermutated UV-associated tumors exhibit remodeled tumor-immune landscapes and biomarker patterns relevant to checkpoint blockade

nodes.description: UV-associated cutaneous angiosarcoma can exhibit remodeled tumor-immune landscapes, including tumor inflammation signatures, tumor-excluded immune signals, and immune biomarkers associated with checkpoint sensitivity. This reflects altered regulation of the local immune response rather than the ultraviolet mutagenesis event itself.

biological_processes: Negative Regulation of T Cell Mediated Immunity

evidence.reference_title: Spatial transcriptomics reveal topological immune landscapes of Asian head and neck angiosarcoma.
evidence.snippet: Spatial transcriptomics revealed topological profiles of the tumor microenvironment, identifying dominant but tumor-excluded inflammatory signals in immune-hot cases

evidence.reference_title: Cutaneous angiosarcoma: A review of current evidence for treatment with checkpoint inhibitors.
evidence.snippet: Like melanoma and cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma, tumor types with known response to immunotherapy, cAS harbors immune biomarkers, such as tumor mutational burden high (TMB-H), PD-L1 positivity, ultraviolet signature expression, and tertiary lymphoid structures.

nodes.description: Angiosarcoma is an endothelial malignancy in which dysregulated angiogenic signaling is a central organizing mechanism. VEGF and related endothelial receptor pathways support pathologic vascular proliferation and cooperate with subtype-specific driver alterations to promote tumor growth and aggressive behavior.

evidence.reference_title: Optimal Clinical Management and the Molecular Biology of Angiosarcomas.
evidence.snippet: We also report the current understanding of the molecular pathways involved in angiosarcoma pathogenesis including MYC amplification, activation of angiogenic pathways and different molecular alterations that are associated with angiosarcomas of different aetiology.

evidence.reference_title: The Angiosarcoma Project: enabling genomic and clinical discoveries in a rare cancer through patient-partnered research.
evidence.snippet: Whole-exome sequencing (WES) of 47 tumors revealed recurrently mutated genes that included KDR, TP53, and PIK3CA.

nodes.description: Radiation-associated and other secondary angiosarcomas frequently show MYC amplification, often alongside additional angiogenesis-related alterations such as FLT4 amplification. This subtype-specific genomic program distinguishes secondary disease from primary angiosarcoma and is associated with multifocal, aggressive breast and chest wall presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Clinicopathologic and molecular correlates to neoadjuvant chemotherapy-induced pathologic response in breast angiosarcoma.
evidence.snippet: By targeted NGS, all 10 post-NACT RA-AS demonstrated MYC amplification, while both primary AS harbored KDR mutations.

evidence.reference_title: Distinct genomic landscapes in radiation-associated angiosarcoma compared with other radiation-associated sarcoma histologies.
evidence.snippet: RT-associated AS had more frequent MYC, FLT4, CRKL, HRAS, and KMT2D alterations than sporadic AS (enriched in TP53, KDR, ATM, ATRX)

nodes.description: Cutaneous angiosarcoma of the head, neck, face, and scalp can show a dominant ultraviolet mutational signature with elevated tumor mutation burden, consistent with ultraviolet-associated mutagenesis as a disease- shaping process in this anatomic subset.

evidence.reference_title: The Angiosarcoma Project: enabling genomic and clinical discoveries in a rare cancer through patient-partnered research.
evidence.snippet: Angiosarcoma of the head, neck, face and scalp (HNFS) was associated with a high tumor mutation burden (TMB) and a dominant ultraviolet damage mutational signature

evidence.reference_title: Spatial transcriptomics reveal topological immune landscapes of Asian head and neck angiosarcoma.
evidence.snippet: UV-positive AS-HN harbored significantly higher tumor mutation burden than UV-negative cases
